occurrence_sentence: "Classical simulation of quantum chemistry problems serves as a goalpost for quantum computers," said Karol Kowalski, a computational chemist at PNNL.

Working with collaborators in Hungary and the Czech Republic, the PNNL team set the benchmark by simulating the structure of an important chemical structure in nitrogenase, an enzyme that converts nitrogen in the atmosphere into useable fertilizer for plants.
"Classical simulation of quantum chemistry problems serves as a goalpost for quantum computers," said Karol Kowalski, a computational chemist at PNNL.

Working with collaborators in Hungary and the Czech Republic, the PNNL team set the benchmark by simulating the structure of an important [chemical structure in nitrogenase](https://www.pnnl.gov/news-media/setting-stage-fuel-efficient-fertilizer), an enzyme that converts nitrogen in the atmosphere into useable fertilizer for plants.

interaction_phrase: working collaboratively with partners at Microsoft
occurrence_sentence: Krishnamoorthy, Kowalski, and their PNNL colleagues are working collaboratively with partners at Microsoft, through the [Northwest Quantum Nexus](https://pnnl-my.sharepoint.com/personal/karyn_hede_pnnl_gov/Documents/ACMDD%20-%20Nathan%20Baker/Quantum%20leadership/nwquantum.com), to both simulate how a quantum computer will work and write programs that will work on any quantum computer that emerges from the intense global competition.

interaction_phrase: working collaboratively with partners at Microsoft
occurrence_sentence: Krishnamoorthy, Kowalski, and their PNNL colleagues are working collaboratively with partners at Microsoft, through the Northwest Quantum Nexus, to both simulate how a quantum computer will work and write programs that will work on any quantum computer that emerges from the intense global competition.
Until a full-scale quantum computer is available, the PNNL team worked with Microsoft experts to develop a bridge between current digital computers and what comes next.
The work described in this feature article was supported by the DOE, Office of Science, Office of Basic Energy Sciences, the Chemical Sciences, Geosciences, and Biosciences Division.
